prompt_md: You own the AI calls across a dev toolchain: **inline code completion** (very high volume), an **automated PR reviewer** (every PR), and a rare **"reason hard about this design"** helper. Cost and latency matter, code output must be repeatable, and only some tasks are genuinely hard. **Pick the best model strategy and the correct reason.**

decision_choices: Use your largest, most capable model for every call site so quality is never a concern
Use a small fast model for completion and routine PR review, route only hard design/debug cases to a reasoning model, and keep temperature low for code tasks
Use one mid-size model everywhere at a high temperature so the output feels creative
Always pick whichever model is newest and use its default settings at every call site
reason_choices: Because matching model capability, cost, and latency to each task — and routing only the hard cases to a stronger model — controls spend and speed while keeping quality where it matters, and low temperature keeps code output repeatable
Because the largest model is always the right default regardless of task or volume
Because a single high-temperature model guarantees consistent, repeatable code output
Because newer models no longer hallucinate and need no per-task configuration
